metadata.description: Challenges validating edge-case handling across all 17 text format parsers: empty files, maximum file sizes, binary content detection, corrupted headers, mixed encodings, nested format embedding, and boundary conditions

challenges.name: Empty File Parsing for All 17 Formats
challenges.description: Verify that all 17 format parsers handle empty string input gracefully without crashing, throwing exceptions, or producing invalid ParsedDocument. Each parser must return a valid ParsedDocument with empty or minimal content, no errors, and a valid toHtml() output.

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse result per format for empty input

outputs.name: formats_crashed
outputs.description: Formats that crashed on empty input

outputs.name: formats_with_errors
outputs.description: Formats that reported errors on empty input

outputs.name: html_valid
outputs.description: Whether toHtml() produced valid output for each format

assertions.message: No format parser may crash on empty input
No format parser should report errors for empty input
All formats must produce valid HTML output from empty input

challenges.name: Whitespace-Only Content Parsing
challenges.description: Verify that all 17 format parsers handle content consisting only of whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines, carriage returns, form feeds) without errors. Test with various whitespace-only patterns including single space, multiple newlines, mixed whitespace, and Unicode whitespace characters.

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse results per format per whitespace pattern

outputs.name: formats_failed
outputs.description: Formats that failed on whitespace-only input

assertions.message: No format parser may fail on whitespace-only input
All parse results must be valid for whitespace-only content

challenges.name: Maximum File Size Handling
challenges.description: Verify that format parsers handle large documents (1MB, 5MB, 10MB) without OutOfMemoryError or excessive parse times. For each supported format, generate documents of increasing sizes and verify that parsing completes within a time budget proportional to file size, and that memory usage stays within bounds.

outputs.name: size_results
outputs.description: Parse time and memory usage per format per size threshold

outputs.name: oom_formats
outputs.description: Formats that threw OutOfMemoryError

outputs.name: timeout_formats
outputs.description: Formats that exceeded the time budget

assertions.message: No format parser may throw OutOfMemoryError for supported file sizes
No format parser may exceed its time budget

challenges.name: Single-Line Document Parsing
challenges.description: Verify that all format parsers handle documents consisting of a single very long line (100KB) without stack overflow, excessive memory usage, or regex catastrophic backtracking. This tests parsers that may rely on line-by-line processing.

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse results per format for single-line input

outputs.name: stackoverflow_formats
outputs.description: Formats that threw StackOverflowError

outputs.name: backtracking_formats
outputs.description: Formats that exhibited catastrophic regex backtracking

assertions.message: No format parser may throw StackOverflowError on long single-line input
No format parser may exhibit catastrophic regex backtracking

challenges.name: Binary Content Detection Accuracy
challenges.description: Verify that FormatRegistry correctly identifies binary content and routes it to the binary format parser. Test with actual binary payloads including PNG headers, ZIP headers, ELF binaries, PDF magic bytes, and random binary data. Also test that text files with occasional high bytes are not falsely detected as binary.

outputs.name: detection_results
outputs.description: Binary detection results for each sample

outputs.name: false_negatives
outputs.description: Binary files not detected as binary

outputs.name: false_positives
outputs.description: Text files falsely detected as binary

assertions.message: All binary content must be detected as binary
No text content should be falsely detected as binary
All detection results must be correct

challenges.name: Corrupted Markdown Header Handling
challenges.description: Verify Markdown parser handles corrupted or malformed headers gracefully: unclosed code fences, mismatched heading levels, broken link syntax ([link(url), deeply nested blockquotes (>>>>>), and truncated YAML front matter (--- without closing ---).

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse results for each corrupted Markdown sample

outputs.name: parser_crashed
outputs.description: Whether the parser crashed on any sample

assertions.message: Markdown parser must not crash on corrupted input
All corrupted inputs must produce a valid ParsedDocument

challenges.name: Corrupted Todo.txt Format Handling
challenges.description: Verify Todo.txt parser handles malformed entries: invalid priority markers ((Z)), invalid dates (2025-13-32), entries with only whitespace between fields, excessively long context/project tags, and mixed line endings within a single file.

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse results for each corrupted Todo.txt sample

outputs.name: parser_crashed
outputs.description: Whether the parser crashed on any sample

assertions.message: Todo.txt parser must not crash on corrupted input
All corrupted inputs must produce a valid ParsedDocument

challenges.name: Corrupted CSV Format Handling
challenges.description: Verify CSV parser handles malformed data: unclosed quotes, inconsistent column counts across rows, embedded newlines in unquoted fields, BOM markers, null bytes in cells, and files with only delimiters and no data.

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse results for each corrupted CSV sample

outputs.name: parser_crashed
outputs.description: Whether the parser crashed on any sample

assertions.message: CSV parser must not crash on corrupted input
All corrupted inputs must produce a valid ParsedDocument

challenges.name: Corrupted LaTeX Document Handling
challenges.description: Verify LaTeX parser handles corrupted documents: missing \end{document}, unmatched braces, invalid command sequences, deeply nested environments (100 levels), and binary garbage after \documentclass.

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse results for each corrupted LaTeX sample

outputs.name: parser_crashed
outputs.description: Whether the parser crashed on any sample

assertions.message: LaTeX parser must not crash on corrupted input
All corrupted inputs must produce a valid ParsedDocument

challenges.name: Mixed Encoding Detection and Handling
challenges.description: Verify that format parsers handle documents with mixed character encodings: UTF-8 with embedded Latin-1 sequences, UTF-16 BOM followed by UTF-8 content, Shift-JIS in a UTF-8 document, and invalid UTF-8 continuation bytes. Parsers must not crash and should produce valid output even if some characters are replaced.

outputs.name: encoding_results
outputs.description: Encoding handling results per format per sample

outputs.name: formats_crashed
outputs.description: Formats that crashed on mixed encoding

outputs.name: replacement_used
outputs.description: Whether Unicode replacement character was used for invalid sequences

assertions.message: No format parser may crash on mixed encoding input
All encoding test results must show graceful handling

challenges.name: Nested Format Embedding: Markdown Inside Org-mode
challenges.description: Verify that Org-mode parser handles content that contains valid Markdown syntax (# headings, **bold**, [links](url)) within Org-mode src blocks or plain text. The Markdown syntax must not confuse the Org-mode parser or corrupt its output.

outputs.name: parse_result
outputs.description: Parse result for Org-mode with embedded Markdown

outputs.name: output_corrupted
outputs.description: Whether the Org-mode output was corrupted by Markdown syntax

assertions.message: Embedded Markdown must not corrupt Org-mode parser output
Parse result must not be empty

challenges.name: Nested Format Embedding: LaTeX Inside Markdown
challenges.description: Verify that Markdown parser handles embedded LaTeX math notation ($E=mc^2$, $$\int_0^1 f(x)dx$$, \begin{equation}...\end{equation}) without corrupting the document structure or crashing on unmatched delimiters.

outputs.name: parse_result
outputs.description: Parse result for Markdown with embedded LaTeX

outputs.name: latex_preserved
outputs.description: Whether LaTeX notation was preserved in output

outputs.name: structure_valid
outputs.description: Whether document structure remained valid

assertions.message: Markdown document structure must remain valid with embedded LaTeX
Parse result must not be empty

challenges.name: Nested Format Embedding: Code Blocks with Format Syntax
challenges.description: Verify that format parsers correctly handle code blocks or verbatim sections that contain syntax from other formats. For example, Markdown code blocks containing Org-mode headers, AsciiDoc listing blocks containing CSV data, or WikiText nowiki blocks containing HTML.

outputs.name: parse_results
outputs.description: Parse results for each nested code block scenario

outputs.name: inner_syntax_leaked
outputs.description: Cases where inner format syntax was interpreted instead of being treated as code

assertions.message: Code block content must never be interpreted as outer format syntax
All nested code block scenarios must parse correctly

challenges.name: Deep Nesting Depth Limits
challenges.description: Verify that format parsers handle extremely deep nesting without stack overflow: 100-level nested Markdown blockquotes, 100-level nested LaTeX environments, 100-level nested Org-mode headings, and 100-level nested WikiText templates. Parsers must either handle the depth or fail gracefully.

outputs.name: nesting_results
outputs.description: Results per format for deep nesting tests

outputs.name: stackoverflow_formats
outputs.description: Formats that threw StackOverflowError

outputs.name: max_safe_depth
outputs.description: Maximum safe nesting depth per format

assertions.message: No format parser may throw StackOverflowError on deep nesting
All deep nesting tests must complete without crashing

challenges.name: ParsedDocument HTML Cache Consistency
challenges.description: Verify that ParsedDocument.toHtml() lazy caching is consistent: the first call generates HTML, subsequent calls return the cached value, and the cached value is identical to the first generation. Test across all 17 formats with documents that include special characters and Unicode.

outputs.name: cache_results
outputs.description: Cache consistency results per format

outputs.name: inconsistent_formats
outputs.description: Formats where cached HTML differs from first generation

outputs.name: cache_hit_verified
outputs.description: Whether cache hit was verified for all formats

assertions.message: Cached HTML must always match first-generated HTML
Cache hit must be verified for all formats
